seoTitle: Use Images in HubSpot Content: Setup Guide
metaDescription: Upload, optimize, and insert images across HubSpot pages, blogs, and emails, including alt text, sizing, and automatic compression settings.
intro: Getting images to look right across HubSpot pages, blogs, and emails means uploading through the Files Manager and configuring a few settings in the content editor. This guide covers dragging the Image module into your layout, adding accessibility alt text, and adjusting sizing so it stays responsive. It ends with a note on HubSpot's built-in compression for faster page loads.

steps.heading: Open your content editor
steps.body: Go to the target page, blog post, or email you want to add an image to.

steps.heading: Find the Image module
steps.body: Look in the left sidebar module panel for the core Image element.

steps.heading: Drop it into your layout
steps.body: Drag and drop the Image module directly into your content canvas.

steps.heading: Upload or select your asset
steps.body: Click the module to open the file selector and upload a fresh image or choose one from your library.

steps.heading: Add alt text and links
steps.body: Input accessibility alt text and set up any redirect links tied to the image.

steps.heading: Adjust sizing and save
steps.body: Set width percentages and margins, save your layout, and check the responsive preview.

faq.question: Why is alt text important for images in HubSpot?
faq.answer: Alt text helps with SEO search rankings and makes your content more accessible to screen reader users.

faq.question: Does HubSpot compress images automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, HubSpot applies automatic image compression to help maintain fast page loading speeds.

faq.question: Can I link an image to another page or URL?
faq.answer: Yes, the image options panel lets you set a redirection destination link on the module.

faq.question: Where are uploaded images stored?
faq.answer: They're stored in your central HubSpot Files Manager, which you can browse when inserting images elsewhere.
